description_long: This is a roughly triangular, medium-sized rock with a coarse, granular texture and a predominantly gray-green coloration. The surface is uneven, with visible mineral grains and some subtle color variations, including hints of beige and darker specks. One face of the rock is relatively flat, while the other sides are more irregular and jagged, suggesting it may have fractured naturally. There is a small, rectangular label with white numbers on a black background affixed to one side, indicating cataloging or collection for study. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or significant weathering. Its size and shape make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A coarse, gray-green triangular rock with a flat face, jagged edges, and a small catalog label on one side. Its granular texture and solid form suggest it is dense and unweathered.
description_view_0: The rock is seen from an angled side, highlighting its irregular, jagged profile and the rough, granular surface.
description_view_1: This view shows the flattest face of the rock, with the catalog label '12388.' clearly visible in the lower left corner.
description_view_2: The rock's broad, flat surface is prominent here, with subtle color variations and a generally even texture.
description_view_3: An angled view emphasizing the rock's thickness and the uneven, sloping edge opposite the flat face.
